growth_records.cite: Madin JS et al. A synthesis of bacterial and archaeal phenotypic trait data. Sci Data 7:170 (2020); source dataset: engqvist.
growth_records.notes: Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

growth_records.cite: Madin JS et al. A synthesis of bacterial and archaeal phenotypic trait data. Sci Data 7:170 (2020); source dataset: fierer.
growth_records.notes: Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

up.method: MIMS Vmax, accelerated from 0.24 to 1.80 mmol/gDW/h (4→30 μmol/min/gDW); Km(app)=0.04-0.10 μM
steady-state net uptake qCH4=D/Yxs at D=0.001 (no H2)
steady-state net uptake qCH4=D/Yxs at D=0.001 (with H2 co-feed)

sec.method: drop in CO2 gas outflow measured on H2 supply (Table S4); absolute per-biomass rate not extractable from main text

yields.Yxs: 2.8 g DW/mol CH4 (no H2); 5.4 g DW/mol CH4 (with H2), at D≈0.001 h−1
yields.product_yields: H2 consumption 6-7 fold higher than CH4 consumption under H2 supply

growth_records.cite: Ida F. Peterse et al. Hydrogen Oxidation Benefits Alphaproteobacterial Methanotrophs Under Severe Methane Limitation. Environmental Microbiology (2025). DOI:10.1111/1462-2920.70163. [PMC12319191]
growth_records.snippet: a similar experiment was performed with the CH4-limited bioreactor culture (D = 0.001 h−1). H2 consumption was instantaneous and 6- to 7-fold higher than CH4 consumption... we calculated a yield increase from 2.8 to 5.4 g DW mol CH4−1
growth_records.notes: CH4-limited chemostat; kinetics at D=0.002 h−1 (Table 1), yield experiment at D=0.001 h−1. μ=D; td at D=0.002 = ln2/0.002=346.6 h. Table 1: Vmax CH4=93 μmol/min/gDW ->5.58 mmol/gDW/h; Vmax H2 4->30 μmol/min/gDW ->0.24->1.80 mmol/gDW/h; Km CH4=3.5 μM, Km H2=0.04-0.10 μM. Yields at D=0.001: 2.8 (no H2), 5.4 (with H2) g DW/mol CH4. qCH4=D/Y at D=0.001: 0.001/2.8=3.57e-4=0.36 mmol/gDW/h (no H2); 0.001/5.4=1.85e-4=0.19 mmol/gDW/h (with H2). OD600=1 = 205.8±5.6 mg DW/L. Culture confirmed contaminant-free by read-based taxonomy. CO2 outflow drop on H2 supply reported qualitatively (Table S4), not as per-gDW flux in main text.
